what_it_does: Defines the C3 node-record contract as Pydantic models: a workflow-face NodeInstance, Edge, and Graph, plus a compiled execution-face ExecNode, along with an additive schema version and a single-source edge-kind registry.
observations: SCHEMA_VER is 2 and the change is additive: Edge.kind defaults to data so old graphs load unchanged.
EDGE_KINDS centralizes the vocabulary for data, injection, gate, and fan_in wires.
NodeInstance carries visual/presentation fields (position, size, render_state, layer, status) while ExecNode strips them.
Outputs are stored as dict[str,str] mapping port names to addresses, both on NodeInstance and ExecNode.
Status is a Literal covering idle through surfaced.
XY and WH have concrete defaults (0,0 and 268x120).
standouts: Additive schema versioning with a default-preserving Edge.kind field.
Explicit edge-kind registry co-located with the model.
Clean separation between the editable canvas record and the pixel-free runtime record.
conventions: Schema-additive evolution: bump SCHEMA_VER and add optional fields, never break existing records.
Single-source vocabulary registry (EDGE_KINDS) paired with a Literal type.
Pydantic BaseModel with default_factory for mutable defaults.
Heavy inline references to external rules/build-prep contracts in docstrings and comments.
Literal types used for status and edge kinds.
concerns: The EDGE_KINDS dict and the EdgeKind Literal are not mechanically linked, so adding a kind requires updating both in sync.
NodeInstance.outputs and ExecNode.outputs are plain string dictionaries with no validation of address format.
There is no visible schema migration or version-gate logic inside this file.
Comments cite external rule identifiers (R1-FOLD, R2-FOLD H5, C1.3, C4.2) that are not defined here.
notes: This file is the shape-of-record contract that keeps the editable canvas graph separate from the compiled runtime node.
questions: Where and how is SCHEMA_VER checked or enforced during load/save?
How are NodeInstance.outputs populated and consumed by the runtime?
What is the exact relationship between NodeInstance.type and the NodeType contract (C2)?
How do gate/fan_in node behaviors coordinate with the edge kind label?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it defines the node record for both workflow and execution faces, and the code does exactly that.
intention_signals: Establish a stable, versioned data contract for canvas nodes and edges that can evolve without breaking existing graphs, while keeping the runtime execution representation separate and pixel-free.
novelty: The explicit edge-kind registry co-located with the Pydantic model, treating edge kinds as a declared, renderable vocabulary rather than implicit wire semantics.
contribution: A versioned, additive schema for graph nodes and edges with a registered edge-kind vocabulary and a clean split between editable workflow records and compiled execution records.
summary_for_embedding: Pydantic models for the C3 node-record contract, covering workflow-face NodeInstance, Edge, Graph, and execution-face ExecNode, plus an additive edge-kind registry defaulting to data wires.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a stable, backward-compatible serialization contract that separates the visual graph from the compiled runtime node representation.

symbols.does: A Pydantic model for 2D canvas coordinates with zero defaults.
symbols.description: Stores x and y as floats, defaulting to (0,0), used to position a NodeInstance on the workflow canvas.

symbols.does: A Pydantic model for width/height dimensions with production defaults.
symbols.description: Stores w and h as floats defaulting to 268x120, representing the rendered size of a NodeInstance.

symbols.does: Workflow-face Pydantic model for an editable node on the canvas.
symbols.description: Captures id, node type, config, visual geometry (XY/WH), render state, layer, runtime status, and output port addresses; it is the pixel-aware, user-editable record.

symbols.does: Workflow-face Pydantic model for a directed connection between ports with a declared kind.
symbols.description: Links from_node/from_port to to_node/to_port and carries an additive EdgeKind defaulting to data, allowing old graphs to load unchanged while supporting injection/gate/fan_in labels.

symbols.does: Workflow-face Pydantic container of nodes and edges.
symbols.description: Holds an id and lists of NodeInstance and Edge records, representing a complete editable canvas graph.

symbols.does: Execution-face Pydantic model for a compiled, pixel-free runtime node.
symbols.description: Strips visual fields and keeps id, type, resolved input port addresses, config, and output addresses; this is what the runtime executes.
